title: lotus scoparius var. traskiae
intro: Lotus scoparius var. traskiae, also known as Trask's lotus or California golden pea, is a rare and endangered variety of the lotus scoparius plant, a type of flowering plant native to the western coast of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Lotus scoparius var. traskiae include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting digestive health and immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and health.
